diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Van der Woude syndrome (VWS) with congenital interalveolar synechiae and cleft palate
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the presence of congenital interalveolar synechiae, cleft palate, and a family history consistent with VWS. Physical examination revealed micrognathia and severely restricted mouth opening. Imaging studies confirmed the absence of masseteric or temporalis scarring or banding, and karyotyping, echocardiogram, head ultrasound, renal ultrasound, and EEG were normal, ruling out other potential causes.
